spec.reasoning: Risk flags indicate the task deletes/inserts rows and depends on blank row positions, so blank_row_insert and title_row_insert are unsafe. I chose lexical rename and distractor_column as the two safe perturbation types. Column rename: 'Current Balance' → 'Outstanding Balance' (same descriptiveness, full phrase to full phrase). Sheet rename: 'imported Data' → 'Loaded Data' (same descriptiveness level, common synonym). The instruction references 'Imported Data' as a sheet name and 'Column C' positionally — the sheet rename forces the agent to detect the new sheet name rather than hardcoding 'Imported Data'. The instruction also says 'Column C' which remains correct positionally. A distractor column 'Running Total' is added after the last column (column D) — it is plausibly financial but clearly different from 'Outstanding Balance'. No data_value_renames needed since the header name does not appear as data values. instruction_paraphrase is false to avoid meaning drift.
